channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Desi Lydic Wants Trump to Take a Hint About the Ballroom
item.description: “Might I suggest: If the ballroom starts experiencing ballistic assault, maybe we just cancel the ball?” she said on “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: SpaceX prepara una oferta pública para salir a la bolsa de valores
item.description: La empresa se ha comprometido a debutar en junio, y Musk aspira a recaudar entre 50.000 y 75.000 millones de dólares con la salida, dijo una de las personas familiarizadas con la compañía.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac, Lauren Hirsch and Maureen Farrell

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times

item.title: Apple cumple 50 años: así recuerda uno de sus primeros empleados
item.description: Cuando tenía 14 años, Chris Espinosa empezó como demostrador de las computadoras hechas en la casa donde creció Steve Jobs. Medio siglo después, con 64 años, sigue en la empresa.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chris Espinosa muestra su primer credencial de empleado de Apple

item.title: New York Is a Hot Spot for Alpha-Gal. Why Doesn’t the State Track Cases?
item.description: In more than 10 states, laboratories or doctors must notify the state health authorities of each positive test for a marker of the syndrome.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Johnny Milano for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Alpha-gal syndrome, which involves an allergy to red meat, is triggered by the bite of a lone-star tick.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for April 2, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Qué significa parasol
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 13 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Semana de la Moda de Tokio: las tendencias que triunfaron
item.description: Aunque no es la que más acapara la atención, la Semana de la Moda de Tokio, que tuvo lugar este mes, puede ofrecer la misma o incluso más inspiración estilística que las pasarelas más populares.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

media:content.media:credit: Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times

item.title: Ciudad del Cabo, la urbe donde el 70% de la vivienda del centro es para turistas
item.description: La escasez de viviendas asequibles en esta ciudad costera de Sudáfrica ha obligado a mucha gente a vivir lejos del centro de la ciudad, mientras que los turistas ocupan inmuebles de primera categoría.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Joao Silva

media:content.media:credit: Joao Silva/The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: parasol
item.description: This word has appeared in 13 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: 5 conclusiones del debate sobre la ciudadanía por derecho de nacimiento
item.description: Los jueces de la Corte de EE. UU. se enfrentaron a cuestiones sobre domicilios y niños expósitos, evitaron debates políticos y reflexionaron sobre el alcance de posibles sentencias.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak and Ann E. Marimow

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El caso argumentado el miércoles se centró en la constitucionalidad de una orden ejecutiva que pretende poner fin a la ciudadanía para los bebés nacidos en suelo estadounidense de migrantes indocumentados y visitantes extranjeros temporales.

item.title: The Only Thing Trump Is Missing in Cuba Is a Plan
item.description: Starving Cubans does nothing to advance American interests.
item.dc:creator: Ricardo Zúniga

media:content.media:credit: Adalberto Roque/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: NASA Launches Artemis Astronauts on Journey to the Moon
item.description: Crowds gathered near the Kennedy Space Center in Cape Canaveral, Fla., on Wednesday to watch as NASA launched four astronauts on a 10-day journey that will go around the moon.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times

item.title: ¿Quiénes son los astronautas que van rumbo a la Luna?
item.description: En un mundo dividido políticamente, cuatro astronautas intentarán mostrar que el esfuerzo, la tenacidad y, sobre todo, la humanidad no tienen fronteras.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Bella and PS Spencer

media:content.media:credit: PS Spencer

item.title: China’s Aiming for the Moon, and NASA Is Looking Over Its Shoulder
item.description: The U.S. space agency launched a lunar flyby Wednesday, but Beijing is pursuing its own space program with formidable focus. Here’s what we know about it, in photos and videos.
item.dc:creator: Selam Gebrekidan and Joy Dong

item.title: Iran War Showcases Strength of South Korean Defense Sector
item.description: Missile interceptors made by the South Korean firm LIG Nex1 are said to be performing well, at a small fraction of the cost of U.S. interceptors.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik and Farah Stockman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Center for Strategic and International Studies

media:content.media:credit: Chris Jung/NurPhoto, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Cheongung-II, a South Korean air defense system, on display last year. The country’s defense companies are rapidly taking on new business, as U.S. firms struggle to keep pace with demand from multiple conflicts.

item.title: Quote of the Day: He Won Birthright Citizenship for All. His Kin Never Knew.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, April 2, 2026.

item.title: Texas Man Charged With Manslaughter in Connection With Cold Case
item.description: Prosecutors in Galveston County say that in the 1980s, a man killed a teenager and helped hide the remains of a woman, two of the dozens of bodies found on land known as the Texas Killing Fields.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Buster Dean/Houston Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Tim Miller, father of the victim Laura Miller, hugging his wife, Georgeann, in 1998 as they looked at one of the billboards seeking information to help solve killings in the Houston area.

media:content.media:credit: NASA, via Associated Press

item.title: 5 Takeaways From Trump’s Address on Iran
item.description: President Trump did not define a clear path out of the conflict, which he estimated would end within three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Alex Brandon
media:content.media:description: President Trump giving a prime-time address from the White House on Wednesday.

item.title: Transcript: Trump’s Speech on Iran War
item.description: The president made his case for the U.S. attack, and said that the main objectives had been achieved.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.description: Nguyen Van Loc performing at a music club in Hanoi, Vietnam, in March.

media:content.media:credit: Linh Pham for The New York Times

item.title: Oil Prices Spike and Stocks Tumble After Trump Vows More Strikes on Iran
item.description: President Trump claimed in a White House address that the war on Iran was “nearing completion” but made no new announcements about the conflict.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Phill Magakoe/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: The Artemis II Toilet Had a Problem
item.description: The Universal Waste Management System aboard the Orion capsule is an innovation in deep space toiletry (it seems to be fixed now).
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Orion training module, an exact replica of the spacecraft that will take astronauts around the moon, at the Johnson Space Center in Houston. A compartment in the floor houses the only bathroom on board.

item.title: For the First Time, a Toilet Heads Into Deep Space
item.description: The Orion spacecraft will take four crew members and one commode around the moon and back as part of the Artemis II mission. In April 2025, National Geographic worked with NASA and filmed the astronauts at an Orion space capsule model in Houston.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An Orion training module, an exact replica of the spacecraft that will take astronauts around the moon, at the Johnson Space Center in Houston. A compartment in the floor houses the only bathroom on board.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 2, 2026
item.description: In Joe Marquez’s wisecracking puzzle, the joke’s on us.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Carl Court/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Wordsmiths will enjoy today’s expertly crafted crossword.

item.title: Trump se adjudica éxito militar en Irán pero no fija un fin a los combates
item.description: El presidente de Estados Unidos afirmó en un discurso que los objetivos militares “están cerca de completarse”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump pronunciando un discurso a la nación desde la Casa Blanca el miércoles.

item.title: Judge Finds Border Officials Violated Previous Order on Warrantless Arrests
item.description: A federal judge in California ordered agents to thoroughly document any future stops in an area spanning 34 counties.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A day laborer in Bakersfield, Calif., in January. A federal judge found on Wednesday that border officials appeared to have violated her earlier order when agents carried out an immigration sweep last year in a Home Depot parking lot in Sacramento.

item.title: Man Threatened to Kill President Trump With Sword, Officials Say
item.description: The man made multiple threats on Facebook and said that the law enforcement agents responding to arrest him would be killed, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of Massachusetts
media:content.media:description: Officials searching the home of a Massachusetts man said they found several blades. The man, who was arrested in connection with threatening to kill the president, also appeared with a sword when agents arrived at his home, officials said.

item.title: U.S. Lifts Sanctions on Venezuela’s New Leader, Delcy Rodríguez
item.description: Removing sanctions would allow Delcy Rodríguez, the country’s acting leader, to conduct business with U.S. companies and potentially meet with President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Trump administration removed Delcy Rodríguez, the acting president of Venezuela, from its sanctions list on Wednesday.

item.title: Eugene Mirman, ‘Bob’s Burgers’ Voice Actor, Seriously Injured in Car Crash
item.description: Mr. Mirman crashed his car on Tuesday in New Hampshire. He remains hospitalized with serious but non-life-threatening injuries.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Amy Sussman/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Eugene Mirman, a comedian and actor known for his role as Gene Belcher on the animated series “Bob’s Burgers,” was hospitalized on Tuesday with serious injuries after crashing his car in New Hampshire.

item.title: Corrections: April 2, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, April 2, 2026.

item.title: Watch Live: President Trump Addresses the Nation
item.description: President Trump gives an update on the war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump has used questions about citizenship for years as a political tool.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission Launches Into Space
item.description: The crew of four — three Americans and one Canadian — reached space on Wednesday on the first crewed journey to the moon since 1972.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Lawsuits Are the New Trump Tactic in the Fight to Overhaul Education
item.description: Trump officials have faced dozens of lawsuits over their aggressive efforts to force change in universities and school districts. Now Trump lawyers are taking schools to court.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender and Alan Blinder

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The University of Pennsylvania’s campus in Philadelphia. The Trump administration won in court this week after suing Penn to obtain lists of Jewish staff for an antisemitism investigation.

item.title: D.H.S. Inspector General Inquiry Focuses in Part on Corey Lewandowski
item.description: The investigation comes as administration officials have fielded complaints about how Kristi Noem’s top adviser, Corey Lewandowski, dealt with companies seeking federal contracts.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz, Tyler Pager, Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Scott Dance

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kristi Noem, the former homeland security secretary, during a House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform hearing this year.

item.title: Key Justices Appear Skeptical of Limiting Birthright Citizenship
item.description: During a Supreme Court hearing on Wednesday, several justices expressed skepticism about the Trump administration’s efforts to bar children of undocumented immigrants born in the United States from automatically becoming Americans.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper and Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The case argued on Wednesday focused on the constitutionality of an executive order seeking to end citizenship for babies born on U.S. soil to undocumented immigrants and temporary foreign visitors.

item.title: When Will NASA’s Artemis II Mission Reach the Moon?
item.description: After orbiting Earth, the Orion capsule will carry the astronauts to the moon and arrive by Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn and Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Silva-Martinez family, who drove from Houston to attend the launch, watching the Artemis II launch at Kennedy Space Center.

item.title: Man Convicted of Aggravated Manslaughter in Police Officer’s Death
item.description: While the jury found Guy Rivera guilty in the death of Detective Jonathan Diller in 2024, they acquitted him of first-degree murder, the most serious charge he faced.
item.dc:creator: Miles G. Cohen

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Guy Rivera, charged in the fatal shooting of Officer Jonathan Diller, listens to opening statements as his trial began at Queens Criminal Court last month.

item.title: What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Wednesday.
item.description: Formidable strikes rocked Tehran early Wednesday morning. Iran launched what its state-aligned media called one of the largest attacks on Israel yet, damaging several cities.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The aftermath of a deadly airstrike in Beirut, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: Medical Examiner Rules That a Rohingya Refugee’s Death Was a Homicide
item.description: An autopsy showed that the man suffered dehydration and hypothermia after Border Patrol agents dropped him off on a cold night in Buffalo. Gov. Kathy Hochul condemned their actions as cruel and inhumane.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ley

media:content.media:credit: Craig Ruttle/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The body of Nurul Amin Shah Alam was placed in a grave in Buffalo in February.

item.title: Map: 7.4-Magnitude Earthquake in Indonesia Raises Tsunami Alerts
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Judson Jones, John Keefe, Joey K. Lee and Bea Malsky

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Trump Initially Laid Out Five Goals for the Iran War. Here’s Where They Stand.
item.description: The United States and Israel have done significant damage to Iran’s military capabilities. But Iran still fires missiles, has nuclear material and coordinates with militias in the region.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A new mural in Valiasr Square in Tehran on Wednesday. A large number of Iran’s ballistic missiles and launchers remain undamaged after a month of airstrikes by the United States and Israel.

item.title: What to Know About Ted Bundy, the Notorious Serial Killer
item.description: On Wednesday, the authorities said DNA evidence had linked Mr. Bundy, who was executed in 1989, to the murder of a 17-year-old Utah girl in 1974.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales

media:content.media:credit: Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Ted Bundy at the second day of jury selection in his murder trial on June 27, 1979, in Miami.

item.title: DNA Confirms Ted Bundy Killed Utah Teen in 1974, Investigators Say
item.description: Mr. Bundy had confessed to killing Laura Ann Aime before he was executed in 1989. Investigators said DNA testing provided conclusive proof.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

item.title: Highlights From the Launch of NASA’s Artemis II Moon Mission
item.description: A giant rocket’s tower of flame lifted three Americans and one Canadian at 6:35 p.m. Eastern on the first crewed journey that will go around the moon since 1972.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times

item.title: Trump Claims Military Success but Offers No Clear Timeline to End Fighting
item.description: In a 19-minute address from the White House, President Trump said the U.S. would hit Iran “extremely hard over the next two to three weeks,” but did not make any revealing announcements.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump addressing the nation from the White House on Wednesday.
